query: Post-fix hybrid verification 1782666796 compare local docs with latest web context
answer: Post-fix hybrid verification involves comparing local documents with latest web context by retrieving specific facts from a local knowledge base and relationship discovery or general queries via global search, as demonstrated in API usage examples where "local" mode handles entity-specific questions using uploaded files while "hybrid" modes combine these sources for broader answers [Context 1|web::15::duckduckgo]. This approach enhances Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) by pulling information from both private documents and external web sources like Brave Search API to ensure responses are grounded in current data rather than just pre-trained knowledge [Context 3|web::6::duckdogo]. Verification processes may also require confirming that specific milestones or entities meet criteria before proceeding, often relying on transparent evidence gathered during the process [Context 4|web::19::duckdogo], though direct technical details about "post-fix hybrid verification" mechanisms are not explicitly defined in the provided text.
